title: Law of the Land
byline: Essays & case notes on property, planning & public law
intro: A commonplace book for the law of land and the buildings upon it. Cases read closely, doctrine taken seriously, and the occasional argument that the courts have mistaken a question of policy for a question of principle.
footerNote: New essays by post — no more than weekly.
disclaimer: Law of the Land offers commentary and information only. Nothing here is legal advice, and nothing here creates a professional relationship. For any actual dispute, consult a qualified lawyer.
